item_scores.question_text:
Are there any standalone school 
eye health programmes or initiatives 
within the intervention area 
(i.e., not part of another school
based health programme)?

Does a curriculum for teacher 
training exist for school 
health and/or eye health?

Does a curriculum for nursing 
training exist for school 
health and/or eye health?

Do any policies or regulations limit 
vision screening by teachers?

Are eye drops 
of eye ointment 
available at the School nurse level?

Are eye drops 
of eye ointment 
available at the Community health level?

Are eye drops 
of eye ointment 
available at the Primary health level?

Are eye drops 
of eye ointment 
available at the Secondary health level?

Are eye drops 
of eye ointment 
available at the Tertiary health level?

Are eye drops 
of eye ointment 
available at the Private pharmacies?

Does a referral pathway exist for 
school health/school eye health

Coverage of children for eye health 
in National Health Insurance
Is school health included in national legislation frameworks?
Is school health/school eye health included in the National Education Policy?
s school health/school eye health included in the Education Sector Plan?
Is there a dedicated school health policy or plan?

Is school health/school eye health 
included in the national health policy?

Is school health/school eye health included 
in the national health strategic plan?

s school health included in the Eye 
Health Policy or Strategic Plan?

Is school health included in the Integrated 
People-Centred Eye Care (IPEC) Plan?

s school health included in any of the 
following policies, strategies or plans:
 • Primary health care
 • Community health 
• Non-Communicable Diseases
 • Neglected Tropical Diseases
 • Ear and Hearing Care
 • Human Resources for Health
 • Mother and child health
 • Sexual and Reproductive Health
 • Nutrition
 •  Social behavioral change 
and communication
 • Water, Sanitation and Hygiene for Schools
 • Other (e.g. Gender and Equity)

Is school health/school eye health included 
in the Disability Policy/Strategy/Plan?

Is school health/school eye health included 
in the Rehabilitation Policy/Strategy/plan?

Is school health/school eye health 
included in the assistive technology 
Policy/Strategy/Plan?

Is school health/school eye health included 
in the Special Needs / Special Education (for 
primary/secondary) Policy/Strategy/Plan?

Is school health/school eye health 
included in the Social Protection 
Policy or Strategy or Plan?

Is there an allocated budget for 
school health / school eye health?

Is there an allocated budget 
for assistive products?
Is there an allocated budget for eye health?

Are eye health or eyeglasses 
included in a national health or social 
protection insurance scheme?

Is there a designated unit or officer 
for school health and/or school eye 
health within the ministry structure?

Is there any mechanism for 
coordination of school health and/
 or school eye health between the 
ministries of health and education?

Is there a coordination mechanism 
for school health and/or school eye 
health within the intervention area?

Are there any school-based specific 
health programmes or initiatives within 
the intervention area? (e.g, , deworming, 
NTDs, Water, Sanitation and Hygiene, 
handwashing, NCDs, nutrition)?

If ‘Yes’: 
Does the program 
include school 
eye health?

Is primary eye care available 
in the intervention area?

Is there a cadre available at the 
primary care level who have the skill 
set required to provide refraction 
services to school age children?

Is there a cadre available at the 
primary care level who have the skill 
set required to detect other eye health 
conditions in school age children?

Are primary eye care services, if 
any, linked with school health or 
a school health program?

Are health screening programmes 
currently being undertaken in the 
community (outside of schools)?

Is secondary eye care available 
in the intervention area?

Is tertiary eye care available in 
the intervention area?

Are there any known factors that 
facilitate (enablers) referral uptake for 
children with eye health conditions?

Are there any indicators related to the 
health of children within the Education 
Management Information System (EMIS)?

Are there any indicators related to the 
eye health within the Health Management 
Information System (HMIS) or District 
Health Information System (DHIS)?

Is there any coordination or data school 
health sharing between education 
and health authorities regarding data 
about health of school children?

Does a monitoring, evaluation, and/
 or supportive supervision framework 
for school health and/or school eye 
health exist in the intervention area?

Are essential health human resources (listed 
below for ease of reference) available to 
implement school health and/or school 
eye health in the intervention area

Does a curriculum exist to train them / 
are they trained on school eye health?

Are there any capacity development needs 
of local health administration personnel 
regarding school health/school eye health?

Are there any capacity development needs 
of local education administration personnel 
regarding school health/school eye health?

Does a supportive supervision system exist 
for school health and/or school eye health?

Are there any policy, regulations or 
directives that guide or inform the 
import, production, procurement or sale 
of spectacle frames and lenses?

Are eyeglasses included on the Ministry 
of Health Essential Supplies List or 
Essential List of Assistive Technologies?

Are eyeglasses already included in 
the government supply chain?

Are there other supply chains for 
optical products in the intervention 
area? (i.e. what is the supply mechanism 
from wholesaler to retail)
Are Children's frames available in the intervention area?

Are the children's frames manufactured 
Locally/Imported 
or Both

Is a Local distributor 
available for the Children's frames?
Are Spherical lenses available in the intervention area?

Are the Spherical lenses manufactured 
Locally/Imported 
or Both

Is a Local distributor 
available for the Spherical lenses?
Are Cylindrical lenses available in the intervention area?

Are the Cylindrical lenses manufactured 
Locally/Imported 
or Both

Is a Local distributor 
available for the Cylindrical lenses?
Are Ready-made distance eyeglasses available in the intervention area?

Are the Ready-made distance eyeglasses manufactured 
Locally/Imported 
or Both

Is a Local distributor 
available for the Ready-made distance eyeglasses?
Are Ready-to-clip lenses and frames available in the intervention area?

Are the Ready-to-clip lenses and frames manufactured 
Locally/Imported 
or Both

Is a Local distributor 
available for the Ready-to-clip lenses and frames?
Are frame sizes adapted to local ethnic facial features available?

Is there any information on willingness 
or capacity to pay for eyeglasses 
in the intervention area?

Does social/national health insurance or 
any other government mechanism cover the 
cost of vision screening and/or refraction?

Does social/national health insurance 
or any other government mechanism 
cover the cost of eyeglasses?

Are anti-biotic eyedrops/ointment part of the 
Ministry of Health Essential Medicines List?

Are anti-biotic eyedrops/ointment 
available in the public sector at 
the primary health care level?

Are anti-biotic eyedrops/ointment 
available in the public sector at the 
secondary health care level?

Are anti-biotic eyedrops/ointment 
available in the public sector at 
the tertiary health care level?

To detect or diagnose and treat school 
age children at school level?
To detect eye conditions at the community health care level?

To detect and manage simple eye conditions 
at the primary health care level?

To diagnose and manage eye conditions at 
the secondary health care level?

To diagnose and manage eye conditions 
at the tertiary health care level?

Is there any evidence that the health of children 
is not prioritised in the intervention area?

Is there any evidence that the health 
needs of girls are not prioritised?

Is there any evidence that the health needs of specific 
ethnic groups in the intervention area are not prioritised?

Is information available on barriers faced by 
children and/or their carers which limit to 
spectacle wear among children which that may 
be applicable to the intervention area?
Fears about wearing eyeglasses
Unacceptable colour, style or quality
Low literacy levels of parents

Lack of information about screening 
and use of eyeglasses
No felt need by parents
Misinformation and misconceptions about eyeglasses
Cultural beliefs and practices about
Eye health and use of eyeglasses

Traditional/religious beliefs prevent or delay 
treatment of children who need eyeglasses
Child’s vision affected by witchcraft
Bullying and teasing
Other stigma
Lack of clear messaging on the benefits of eyeglasses

Lack of clear communication on the processes 
involved in a school eye health programme

Fear of loss of earning/daily wages by 
parents to access eyeglasses

Limited availability of financial resources 
in family to procure eyeglasses
Travel cost to point of referral
Cost of eyeglasses
Cost of eye care treatment
Long distance to point of referral to obtain eyeglasses
Long distance to point of referral for eye care treatment
Delay in dispensing of eyeglasses

Lack of person to accompany child to referral 
centre for eyeglasses or eye care treatment

Waiting time between screening and eye care 
treatment or provision of eyeglasses

Seasonal variation that limits access 
to eye health services

Lack of trust by parents in personnel 
conducting screening

Lack of trust by parents in eye care 
service provider at referral centre
Children with disabilities cannot access screening
Children with disabilities cannot access referral centre
Other

Are there barriers within the health or education 
system that prevent uptake of eyeglasses and / 
or eye health services by school age children?
Screeners (e.g. teachers or nurses) inadequately trained
Teachers do not have time to screen

Lack of eye care professionals to 
receive and attend to referrals

Poor attitude / behaviours of education 
personnel at the point of screening

Poor attitude and behaviours of eye health 
personnel at referral centres

No eyeglasses suitable for children 
available within the intervention area
Inadequate state investment in school health services

Eyeglasses not included in health insurance 
or other state financing mechanism

Poor integration of school eye health in 
other health screening programmes

Lack of support services for children 
with vision impairment

Inadequate referral pathway between 
schools and eye health services
Delays in providing eyeglasses
Other

metadata.description: Binary scoring data for determinants analysis
